---
title: "Week 3: Intro to modelling hypotheses"
author: "Brendan Barrett"
format: html
editor: visual
---
# What is science
# How is science related to statistics?
Big world vs. small worlds
- simplification
- we sample from a populations and cannot know the truth of the iniverse
# The four stages of scinetific inquiry
## Observations
This is often refered to as Tinbergen's zeroth question.
Observations start with field observations, observing animals and plants in the wild or captivity, reading the observations of others and 
## Questions
- Questions arise from hypotheses and thoughts, and speculate about what are the underlying - reasons for our hypotheses.
- Refining the scale and detail of the questions is important
- Theory can be importnat in guiding and refining questions
## Hypotheses
- Here is where we (typically) make a causal asserrtion, and propose a plausible and we of reason for the question(s) at stake
- Having multiple hypotheses is good, informed by our understanding of a natural system
## Alternative hypotheses
- are mutually exclusive
- need to think of what to measure, what experiments need to be done and what models to build to tease these apart
## Complementary Hypotheses
- need not be mutually exclusive, but may both be true
## Null Hypotheses
- goes back to Popper and Fisher
- default hypothesis (i.e. no change) or a change near zero
- never accept the null! although commonly done (talk about popper)
- discuss historical tradition

## Hypothesized data generative mechanism
- verbal reasoning
- causal diagrams DAGs
- generative models (linking theory to data)

## Predictions
- Conditional on H1, I predict A and B 
- Conditional on H2 I predict C
- Conditional on H3 I predict C and B
- Multiple hypotheses can correspond to multiple predictions and vice-versa
- 

# Model buillding in the realm of scientific inquiry
- test simple associations (i.e. means)
- make inferences based off of complex causal webs
- fit a mechanistic model as a statistical model to data

# Understanding Causality and Making Predictions

# What is truth
- p-values
- credible inervals
- a continuum of evidence
- there is no one right way, you have to justify it
- stats:prbability theory as engineering:physics
## Quarto

Quarto enables you to weave together content and executable code into a finished document. To learn more about Quarto see <https://quarto.org>.

## Running Code

When you click the **Render** button a document will be generated that includes both content and the output of embedded code. You can embed code like this:

```{r}
1 + 1
```

You can add options to executable code like this

```{r}
#| echo: false
2 * 2
```

The `echo: false` option disables the printing of code (only output is displayed).
